summary: Iter 44 — Verified v2.7.02 fix for the 'two blue focus rings' bug via code review and partial UI test. The setFocusAttr in /app/frontend/src/hooks/useSpatialFocus.js L289-328 has been correctly updated to perform a document-wide sweep of all [data-focused="true"] elements (clearing any except the new target) plus cleanup of [data-holding=true] and [data-pressed=true] stragglers — exactly the fix needed to handle cross-component priming from Home.jsx/Detail.jsx/Settings.jsx/WatchTogether.jsx that bypasses the lastFocused closure variable. index.css L283 restored .vesper-eyebrow color to var(--vesper-blue). ContinueWatchingShelf 'X LEFT' caption restored to brand blue. UI test partially completed — script ran but stdout was truncated to the navigation banner only; screenshots confirm app reached profile picker with 2 pre-existing profiles (Tester + Kids), with Kids appearing focused first (data-initial-focus on second profile). Click on [data-testid='profile-p1'] did not advance past the picker in this run.

issues: Brief states .vesper-eyebrow should compute to rgb(0, 184, 255) but the actual --vesper-blue CSS variable in index.css L21 is #5dc8ff = rgb(93, 200, 255). The CSS restoration to var(--vesper-blue) is correct, but the brief's expected RGB value is stale/wrong. Also note --vesper-blue (#5dc8ff) is the SAME cyan-blue family as --vesper-blue-bright (#8de0ff = focus ring color at L442/450). Restoring brand-blue eyebrows may reintroduce visual competition with the focus ring — the very bug v2.7.01 tried to mute. Worth confirming with the user that brand-blue eyebrows is the desired final visual state.

action_items: Confirm with user whether the brand-blue eyebrow restoration is desired given that --vesper-blue and the focus ring color are in the same cyan-blue family. The original v2.7.01 complaint was 'two blue highlights' — now with eyebrows back to vesper-blue, the same visual could appear when focusing a tile.
Update brief's expected eyebrow RGB from rgb(0, 184, 255) to rgb(93, 200, 255) (matches actual --vesper-blue=#5dc8ff in index.css).

Investigate why clicking [data-testid='profile-p1'] from the profile picker did not advance to /home in the latest test environment (possible regression from a different code path — was working in iter43).
critical_code_review_comments: useSpatialFocus.js L289-328 — setFocusAttr fix is structurally correct: O(n) document.querySelectorAll('[data-focused="true"]') sweep clears all stale attrs except the new target. Same for [data-holding=true], [data-pressed=true]. Logic is sound: the lastFocused closure-tracked element is now redundant (only used by focusEl for the prevVs scroller check at L374-376) but harmless. The fix preserves the focusEl crossing-scrollers behavior.

useSpatialFocus.js L660, L671, L678 — tryPreferred/tryFallback both call setFocusAttr — good, these initial-focus paths use the new sweep. But the components Home.jsx/Detail.jsx/Settings.jsx/WatchTogether.jsx still set data-focused directly during their own priming (per brief). With the new sweep, the first global ArrowDown/ArrowRight from useSpatialFocus will clean those up. The fix is reactive (cleans on first nav) rather than proactive (preventing the dual-attr at mount-time). Acceptable trade-off but worth noting.

index.css L21 --vesper-blue=#5dc8ff and L22 --vesper-blue-bright=#8de0ff are both cyan-blue. Focus ring uses --vesper-blue-bright (L442/450), eyebrows now use --vesper-blue (L283). These ARE distinguishable when side-by-side (5dc8ff vs 8de0ff) but on a low-PPI HK1 projector they will read as 'two blue elements on screen'. Consider whether muted-white eyebrows (the v2.7.01 design) was actually the correct UX call and the dual focus-ring bug should be fixed orthogonally via the setFocusAttr sweep alone.
ContinueWatchingShelf.jsx L378-389 — 'X LEFT' caption also reverted to var(--vesper-blue). Same observation as eyebrows.

success_rate.frontend: code-review PASS for P0 setFocusAttr sweep + P1 brand-blue restore + P2 z-index lift; UI runtime verification of the data-focused invariant was interrupted by profile-picker navigation issue and stdout truncation.

test_credentials: n/a — same localStorage seed as iter43. Note: profile picker now shows 2 pre-existing profiles (Tester=p1 + Kids) — previous runs may have persisted Kids into shared localStorage. Initial focus appears to land on the Kids tile (data-initial-focus=true on Kids?), so the test seed needs to explicitly click Tester by name rather than relying on activeElement after profile-p1 click.
seed_data_creation: Re-seeded onnowtv-profiles-v1=[p1 Tester], active=p1, CW with two entries (Bride!, Shawshank). Pre-existing Kids profile was not wiped.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CRITICAL: When seeding profiles, MERGE existing profiles or explicitly DELETE the Kids entry first — otherwise the picker initial-focus may land on Kids and dispatching Enter or 'click' on [data-testid=profile-p1] may not advance to /home cleanly. Better seed: localStorage.setItem('onnowtv-profiles-v1', JSON.stringify([{id:'p1',name:'Tester',...}])) — this REPLACES the array. Also consider await page.click('[data-testid=profile-p1]') with force=True or page.evaluate("document.querySelector('[data-testid=profile-p1]').click()"). The two screenshots both show profile picker still visible after the click attempt — investigate this regression. Once on /home, the P0 verification script (document.querySelectorAll('[data-focused=true]').length after each programmatic ArrowKey dispatch) should work as designed — the v2.7.02 setFocusAttr sweep is structurally sound (code-reviewed).
rca of the issue: Pre-fix v2.7.01: setFocusAttr only cleared the closure-tracked lastFocused element. Multiple page components (Home.jsx L142-152 etc.) primed their own initial focus by setting data-focused='true' directly on a tile — bypassing useSpatialFocus.setFocusAttr entirely. When the user D-pad-navigated to a tile in a DIFFERENT shelf (handled by the global engine), the global engine would set data-focused on the new tile but the closure-tracked lastFocused was still null or referenced an unrelated past target. The originally-primed tile retained its data-focused attribute → CSS box-shadow rule fired on BOTH tiles → user saw two blue rings. v2.7.02 fix: replace lastFocused-only clearing with a document.querySelectorAll('[data-focused="true"]') sweep. O(n) over a tiny set (typically 1-2 elements), zero perf cost, robust against any code path that sets data-focused directly. Code is structurally correct (verified via L289-328 read). UI runtime verification was attempted but blocked by profile-picker advance regression in the test harness.
